days.title: Tuesday, June 30, 2026
tldr: **Two Greater Bay Area embodied-AI firms crossed a 20 billion yuan post-money valuation on the same day, June 29: X Square Robot (B+/B++/C all closed) and AI² Robotics (nearly 5B yuan in fresh money).** Both claim to be the first in the region past that mark, and the more interesting half of the X Square story is the supply side: it has built its own data-collection factory with a full automated pipeline for gathering, cleaning, labelling and QC, and says its XR Zero G0 capture device cuts the data cost of model training by 95%. X Square via Gasgoo

**Gasgoo ran the clearest piece yet on the embodied-AI chip split, and the argument is that TOPS is the wrong number: robots need latency determinism, not throughput, which is forcing a "big brain, small brain" hardware divide.** 43.8B yuan flowed into Chinese embodied AI in H1 2026, over half into the "embodied brain," while a separate cerebellum-chip race plays out on mature nodes where domestic firms can actually win. Gasgoo

**ViTai Robotics, the MIT CSAIL visuo-tactile spinout, closed a Pre-A (Redbird Navigation led) and is putting the money into a visuo-tactile data-acquisition system plus model closed-loop, so robots learn what to do the moment they touch something.** Xiaomi has backed every round and is running ViTai's fingertip sensors on its own phone production lines. Third round in a year, several hundred million yuan total. Gasgoo

days.title: Monday, June 29, 2026
tldr: **General Intuition raised $320M at a $2.3B valuation on a single contrarian claim: that gameplay video, not real-world capture, is the most underrated robot training data there is.** The clips come with embedded action labels, the exact button a player pressed and when, and the company showed a model that watched 100 hours of a Fortnite-like game then drove a quadruped around an office after eight minutes of real-world fine-tuning. The round closed June 25 and went uncovered here, which is the kind of miss worth correcting because it is a direct argument against the real-capture thesis. TechCrunch

**AGIBOT rolled its 15,000th robot off the line on June 28, the milestone unit a G2 industrial task robot, and Omdia put it first in the world for humanoid shipments in 2025 at 5,168 units and 39% share.** The 10,000-to-15,000 run is the part that is new. The framing from AgiBot is explicit: the competition has moved past single-robot demos to batch delivery and real deployment. PR Newswire via Manila Times

**SENAD closed a 300M yuan Series C on June 25 for embodied logistics, paired with a new Insight-World V3.0 model built on a five-layer architecture with Tsinghua's intelligent-perception lab.** Another Chinese embodied firm funding an on-device world model grounded in a specific vertical, this time warehouse and industrial logistics. Gasgoo

days.title: Sunday, June 28, 2026
tldr: **The biggest open robot-data release of the year landed: ABC-130K, 3,553 hours of bimanual teleoperation across 134,806 episodes and 195 tasks, with the hardware, training code, simulator and evaluation rubrics all open-sourced.** A Berkeley, MIT, Amazon FAR and XDOF team (Allshire, H.G. Singh, R. Singh, Abbeel, Malik, Kanazawa) shipped the whole stack, not just weights. The data is teleop, lab-collected and Western, which is the part worth reading. abc.bot

**CATL took a global strategic stake in the embodied stack, pairing with Galbot to put the Galbot S1 into its own battery factories and to build what they call the first aftermarket service network for humanoids.** The body runs on CATL cells rated at parts-per-billion failure and is already doing material transport and component picking on battery-pack lines, not a demo. A battery giant is vertically integrating into robots. Gasgoo

**DaxAI Robot closed a fresh round led by GSR Ventures and Seas Capital to scale an on-device world model, and its humanoid starts at 69,800 yuan.** DaxBrain-WM runs inference on the edge with sub-50ms action latency and a perception layer that fuses vision, touch, force and proprioception. Another Chinese firm funding the world-model line over cloud VLA, this time priced for mass deployment. Gasgoo

days.title: Saturday, June 27, 2026
tldr: **ANYVERSE DYNAMICS closed an angel round over $200M on June 26, and the tell is the architecture: it walked away from VLA in favour of a latent-space world model plus reinforcement learning.** The Chinese general-robotics firm was co-led by a JD.com-affiliated fund, C Capital and Hony Capital, is closing a separate near-$200M Pre-A, and has nearly $100M in global orders with its K15 robot in mass production. A $200M angel cheque on a non-VLA bet is the clearest sign yet that Chinese embodied-AI capital is hedging the model layer. Gasgoo

**Zhicheng AI raised a fresh round the same day and open-sourced Chengling, which it calls the first end-to-end JEPA-architecture physical world model built for robotics.** The Hangzhou startup, founded March 2024, paired the raise with TR4 Pro and TR5 Pro humanoid upgrades and claims nearly 1.5B yuan in orders. Two China rounds in one day, both backing world models over the VLA consensus, is a pattern worth naming. Gasgoo

**Alibaba launched the Qwen-Robot series, its first full embodied-model family, splitting the stack into manipulation, navigation and a world model.** Qwen-RobotManip is the VLA, Qwen-RobotNav the VLN, Qwen-RobotWorld the world model, all under the Qwen ecosystem and already in pilot with Alibaba Cloud enterprise clients. A hyperscaler productizing the embodied stack as three named models is the embedded-AI move of the week, logged here for the first time. Gasgoo

days.title: Thursday, June 25, 2026
tldr: **MWC Shanghai 2026 opened with humanoid robots walking the stage before any human spoke, and the GSMA's framing is the real signal: the mobile industry says its job is now enabling intelligent machines, not connecting people.** Director General Vivek Badrinath put 5G-Advanced forward as the nervous system for humanoids, drone fleets and autonomous vehicles, and the concrete deal on the floor is China Telecom working with AgiBot on a Robotics-as-a-Service model for aviation and logistics. A state telco is putting its network behind a humanoid fleet. Tech Times

**Automate 2026 closed in Chicago, and the day-three through-line was the orchestration and software layer, not the bodies.** Rockwell launched FactoryTalk Orchestration, InOrbit ran multi-vendor robot orchestration demos, and Vention shipped a digital-twin platform with FANUC and Teradyne. The hardware was settled by Tuesday. The fight on the last day was about who coordinates a mixed fleet. Robotics 24/7

**Berkeley's Abbeel and Malik group put out "Do as I Do," which turns ordinary monocular RGB human videos into dexterous-hand manipulation data and ships a playbook for collecting that data.** This is the data-collection wedge stated as an algorithm: reconstruct hand-object interaction from in-the-wild egocentric and exocentric clips, retarget to a multi-fingered robot hand, get robot-complete trajectories. arXiv

days.title: Wednesday, June 24, 2026
tldr: **Robot.com launched R-noid at Automate 2026, a general-purpose wheeled humanoid sold only as a service, and the pitch is speed-to-deploy, not the body.** Dual 7-DoF arms, a 4-DoF torso, a holonomic base, an onboard VLA model, 19 deployable tasks across five roles, and a claim of first-site-visit to autonomous operation in 8-12 weeks. Paying lighthouse deployments are already live (a Packer at a golf course, another advancing at a food manufacturer). The robot is the easy part now, the moat is the software stack plus how fast you can stand it up. Robotics 24/7

**ABB gave the first public showing of a Physical AI Toolchain, a major industrial vendor productizing the data-and-validation layer end to end.** The stack spans data generation, training and validation, deployment and optimization, sitting on top of ABB's NVIDIA-built RobotStudio HyperReality (claimed ~99% sim-to-real). The validation step being a named, sold product line is the signal: the messy capture-and-check work the thesis keeps flagging is becoming a vendor SKU. Robotics 24/7, Tech Times

**China's dexterity layer is consolidating upward: LinkerBot, the Linker Hand dexterous-hand leader, took a shareholder stake in a new Beijing embodied-AI and data-platform firm.** Zhixing Embodied (Beijing), set up in 2026, lists AI public data platforms and intelligent-robot R&D in its scope, and LinkerBot just merged with a bionic-hand team (Jingling Zhikang) in May. A hardware specialist is buying its way into models and data rather than staying a parts supplier. Gasgoo

days.title: Tuesday, June 23, 2026
tldr: **Bear Robotics is acquiring Kinisi Robotics, and the asset it is actually buying is a tactile-data-collection capability, not a humanoid.** A service and AMR company is folding in Kinisi's KR1 wheeled humanoid, its Bristol engineering team, its VLA and robot-foundation-model manipulation stack, and specifically its low-cost gripper and glove tech built to gather tactile training data. Founder Brennan Pierce becomes Chief Robotics Officer. A deployment company that did not have a capture pipeline just bought one rather than build it. The Robot Report, press release

**Google's Intrinsic debuted an "Intelligence Cell" to kill manual robot programming, which is the software-is-the-bottleneck thesis shipping as a product.** Intrinsic, now folded into Google, put out an AI-powered cell that determines tasks and runs a workcell without hand-written robot code, landing the same week QNX research named software the biggest blocker to physical AI. The Robot Report

**US robotics policy moved, with the National Commission on Robotics Act advancing through both chambers.** Bipartisan Senate and House bills (S.4686, H.R.7334) would stand up an 18-member commission under Commerce to assess US robotics competitiveness, framed squarely around dependence on foreign-built robots and supply-chain security. Governance is starting to treat the robot stack as strategic infrastructure. The Robot Report, McCormick Senate

days.title: Monday, June 22, 2026
tldr: **Thin day, and the brief should say so rather than pad it.** Weekend run. The arXiv cs.RO listing has not posted a new batch past June 15 (all already covered), the monitored X handles were pinned or stale across the board, China was quiet, and no net-new funding round cleared the window. Nothing below is manufactured to fill space.

**The one fresh-ish money item is Addverb planning a $100M+ raise that names data collection as a use of funds.** Reliance-backed, first raise since 2021, earmarked for humanoids, quadrupeds, in-house lidar and AI training, on ~$136M revenue and a ~$200M order book. It surfaced June 11, logged here for the first time. A body-maker reaching down into the data layer. Business Today
**Standing correction, one line: retire the "no local capture entrant" claim.** Human Archive has been operating since late May, so that line in earlier briefs was wrong. Noted once, not re-reported.

days.title: Saturday, June 20, 2026
tldr: **Physical Intelligence and NVIDIA's Cosmos team published a way to evaluate robot policies without running them in the real world, and it is the data-validation problem stated as a research result.** SC3-Eval adapts a pretrained video foundation model into a policy evaluator by enforcing three kinds of self-consistency (forward-inverse dynamics, cross-view, and a test-time uncertainty signal), and reaches a 0.929 closed-loop Pearson correlation with real-world success across seven VLA policies. The part that matters for a capture business is that the evaluator only earns that correlation by being anchored to a physically plausible action manifold, which is bought with clean real-world data, not generated frames alone. arXiv 2606.18610, project page, Physical Intelligence

**Built Robotics is putting a purpose-built data-collection robot on construction sites to train a world foundation model, and the spec it names is exactly the capture-and-validate bundle the thesis argues for.** With Penn's xLAB, Built is fielding a sensor-rig robot to collect "diverse personnel and environment data" past what its working machines see, specifically edge cases like odd body poses, occlusions, weird lighting and unexpected human behavior, then "systematically collecting and labeling" them. A Western field operator built a dedicated rig for capture, not a body, and pointed it at validated edge-case data. The Robot Report

**After two weeks of this brief running on China, the Western signal this run is a cluster of production-validated deployments and one non-humanoid entrant.** Sanctuary AI ran physical AI to a production-ready wire-plugging task at a Tier 1 automotive supplier, Autonomique moved semi-humanoids from pilot to production at a Canadian Tier 1, and Genesis AI launched Eno, a wheeled two-armed general-purpose robot on its GENE foundation model with production targeted for end of 2026. The West's week is deployment proof and a deliberate bet against humanoid form, not a new demo. Sanctuary AI, Genesis Eno

days.title: Friday, June 19, 2026
tldr: **The sharpest research drop of the day is a manipulation policy that throws out the 2D-frame assumption every VLA and world-action model is built on, and runs 55× faster for it.** KAIST AI and ETH Zurich posted the Geometric Action Model (GAM), a language-conditioned policy that repurposes a pretrained 3D geometric foundation model as one shared substrate for perception, future prediction and action, instead of bolting action onto a 2D image backbone. It reports being more accurate, more robust and 55× faster than foundation-model-scale baselines. The interesting part for a data business is what it implies about the input: contact-rich skill needs 3D geometry captured at source, not inferred after the fact. arXiv 2606.17046, project page

**An Indian humanoid startup printed a seed round, which is the rarest line in this brief: a local datapoint to put against the daily China cluster.** General Autonomy, founded by ex-ShareChat cofounders Farid Ahsan and Bhanu Pratap Singh, raised about ₹32 Cr (~$3.3M) led by Elevation Capital and India Quotient at a ~₹280 Cr (~$33M) valuation, to build humanoids and autonomous mobile robots for labour-intensive workflows. Small money against the Chinese rounds, but it is a body-maker, not a data layer, which is the same inversion the thesis keeps flagging. Inc42, Dealroom

days.title: Thursday, June 18, 2026
tldr: **A Chinese lab open-sourced a VLA model that it claims tops NVIDIA's GR00T and Physical Intelligence's π₀.₅ on a humanoid benchmark, and the part that matters is what sits under it: a data-collection-first paradigm.** ACE Robotics and CUHK's Multimedia Lab released ACE-Ego, a "one-brain, multi-type" embodied operation model, reporting a 72.8% average success rate on RoboCasa GR1 TableTop (ahead of GR00T, π₀.₅ and JD's JoyAI-RA) and 90.62% on the dual-arm RoboTwin 2.0 under domain randomization. The model is built on ACE's "environmental data collection to Kaiwu World Model 3.0 to embodied interaction" stack, the same company whose home-generator and Angel+ round we covered two days ago. Treat the benchmark numbers as vendor-reported. Gasgoo, ACE-Ego

**A top-five humanoid maker tied itself to a domestic chip vendor and named the goal as closing the "data-model-chip" loop, which is the embedded wedge stated plainly.** Leju Robot (fourth globally in 2025 humanoid shipments per Omdia and Counterpoint) signed with Huaxi Intelligence to co-develop domestic high-compute main-control boards and edge deployment of embodied models. Huaxi's RISE platform runs its in-house R1 chip, in mass production since October 2025 and already inside the Zhiyuan Genie G2. China keeps wiring the silicon layer under the bodies, on domestic parts. Gasgoo, Leju x Huaxi

**A Nasdaq-listed automaker launched a four-robot product family on the same day, a reminder that the new entrants are now coming from the car industry, not the labs.** Faraday Future held its "All-Form FFEAI Robot World" event on June 17, launching the Futurist humanoid (2027 model), the FXNavi quadruped, and the FFMasterMini and FFNova robots, after delivering a Master humanoid to a Los Angeles dental clinic on June 5 and signing a 23-unit order with a K12 education group on May 26. Gasgoo, Faraday Future robots

days.title: Wednesday, June 17, 2026
tldr: **NVIDIA's GEAR lab put frontier coding agents in charge of improving real robots, and they hit a 99% success rate with the humans mostly out of the loop. This is the autoresearch loop arriving in the physical world.** ENPIRE (NVIDIA, CMU, UC Berkeley) is a harness that hands Codex, Claude Code and Kimi Code a fleet of real robots plus an auto-reset and auto-verify interface, and lets them propose algorithms, run rollouts, read papers and refine until tasks like zip-tie cutting and GPU insertion reach 99% pass@8. The piece we care about is the verifier. The reward is a detector plus segmentation fusing per-camera binary verdicts, automated outcome QC standing in for human judgment. ENPIRE, NVIDIA GEAR

**JD turned its crowd-capture program into a physical institution, and named the data vendor underneath it.** On June 16 Yiren Technology, JD, China Machinery Engineering Group and Suqian High-tech Zone launched an Embodied AI Research Institute built around a "large-scale data collection" model, opening standardized capture to students, residents, factory workers and the flexible workforce, with a Suqian center that already holds nearly 10,000 concurrent operators. Yiren is described as a major China embodied-data player doing collection, annotation and simulation training. China keeps building the capture institution. Nobody local to us has one. Gasgoo, JD Suqian institute

**A VC read the Figure livestream as robotics' ChatGPT moment for capital, and said out loud where the money flows next: data collection.** Andrew Kang's post, reposted by Brett Adcock, called the FigureAI stream "a mini ChatGPT moment for venture capital" and predicted the new investor interest will "flow down to data collection, supply chain build out, and a lot more." When the body-makers' own backers say the capital flows down to the data layer, that is the thesis stated by the other side. Andrew Kang via Brett Adcock

days.title: Tuesday, June 16, 2026
tldr: **A Chinese embodied-AI startup raised a fresh round to build a synthetic version of the exact thing we collect, Chinese home environments, and that is the sharpest signal of the day.** ACE Robotics (Daxiao Robot) closed an Angel+ round on June 15 backed by Geely Capital, Shenzhen Capital Group and SenseTime's Guoxiang Capital, putting it past "hundreds of millions of dollars" raised in H1 2026. The money funds its Kaiwu World Model and a "human-centric data acquisition strategy", and the new piece is Kairos-HomeWorld with CUHK's Multimedia Lab, a framework that generates full interactive 3D Chinese homes in one click as a training ground for embodied AI. China is moving to manufacture the environments it cannot otherwise capture at volume. Gasgoo, ACE Robotics Angel+

**Huawei put its operating system inside a humanoid, which adds a third contender to the body-brain-ecosystem fight we covered last week.** At Huawei's HDC 2026 on June 15, Noetix Robotics unveiled the N2, the first consumer humanoid running OpenHarmony, with Richard Yu framing it as the robotics face of "OpenHarmony in every home" and a "Hundred People, Hundred Machines" developer program shipping free units to 100 teams. The NVIDIA-versus-Unitree platform debate now has a software-OS entrant that is neither. Gasgoo, Noetix N2 OpenHarmony

**A self-driving company and a robotics company merged their data pipelines, which is the convergence everyone talks about actually printing as a deal.** MINIEYE and LDROBOT announced a partnership (June 11) to build one cross-platform data loop spanning vehicles and robots, a shared Physical AI foundation model, and joint demos in Shenzhen and Changsha. The interesting line is the unified data loop across indoor and outdoor, structured and unstructured scenes. The AD-to-robot data bridge is a real strategy now, not a slide. Gasgoo, MINIEYE x LDROBOT

days.title: Monday, June 15, 2026
tldr: **The biggest physical-AI round on record printed in Europe, not SF or Shenzhen, and the founder said the quiet part out loud: AI infrastructure companies no longer have to come from Silicon Valley.** Germany's NEURA Robotics is raising up to $1.4B in Series C, led by Tether with Qualcomm, Amazon, NVIDIA, Bosch, Schaeffler and the European Investment Bank in. The money goes to humanoid manufacturing, an open robot-learning ecosystem (Neuraverse), and a network of real-world training environments (NEURA Gyms). The deployment plan names Germany and India, targeting millions of robots by 2030. The Robot Report, NEURA Series C

**A Chinese lab open-sourced the exact capture stack we keep describing, ego camera plus dual wrist cams, robot-free, with a quality-inspection loop bolted on, and proved 10 human episodes plus 1 robot episode matches a pure-robot dataset.** X Square Robot released XRZero-G0 and a 2,000-hour G0-Dataset on June 11, claiming up to 20x reduction in real-robot data needs. The rig is now an open recipe, which means the moat moves entirely to whose captured hours survive the inspection step. The Robot Report, XRZero-G0, arXiv 2604.13001

**The Indian press started calling HuPa's thesis by name. "Robo-BPO" is now a category in mainstream Indian business coverage.** Business Standard frames physical-AI data services, the collection, annotation and governance of real-world footage, as India's fourth outsourcing era after voice, IT and KPO. Good for legitimacy, a warning on timing. Business Standard, BPO to robo-BPO

days.title: Sunday, June 14, 2026
tldr: **China spent the weekend turning embodied-data collection into a public utility, and the clearest move is Daimon Robotics and China Mobile building a crowdsourced capture network out of retail stores.** The two announced a partnership on June 10 to use China Mobile's hundreds of thousands of offline stores as a distributed collection grid, with the first site, billed as the world's first "5S store" for embodied data, opening in Chenzhou, Hunan on July 15. The plan starts with 1,000 equipment sets and targets 1 million hours of real-world data a year, with ordinary citizens wearing two-finger grippers, tactile gloves and head-mounted cameras across home, logistics and manufacturing scenes. The capture method is exactly the ego-plus-hands stack we keep saying is the scalable middle of the data pyramid. The news is that a telco is now the distribution layer for it. Gasgoo, crowdsourced data collection

**The Chinese state put its name on the data problem, jointly with two ministries, and called real-world machine data the "oil" of embodied intelligence.** On June 12 the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology and SASAC launched a 2026 national campaign for real-world humanoid training, mandating deployment across 10 key provinces and central SOEs, condensing 100+ high-value scenarios and driving toward 10,000-unit deployment by year-end. The document states the bottleneck plainly. Usable real-world data is short of generalization needs by "at least two orders of magnitude", the early training grounds "lacked unified standards, particularly in data collection", and fragmented data across companies is "like people speaking different dialects". That is the validation-and-standards gap stated by a government, not a vendor. Gasgoo, MIIT and SASAC campaign

**India has no equivalent of any of this, and the gap is the story for us.** While China runs a state plus JD.com plus China Mobile mobilization measured in millions of hours, the most on-thesis Indian robotics round this cycle was Integra's $1.12M pre-Series A, and Indian robotics raised $45M+ across all of H1 2026. Nobody in India is building a national ego-capture grid, which is both the warning and the opening. The distribution gap (Indian hands, homes, hospitals, factories) stays unfilled while China industrializes its own. Indian Startup News, Integra

days.title: Thursday, June 11, 2026
tldr: **China spent the day arguing about who actually owns a humanoid, and the answer is splitting three ways: the body, the brain and the ecosystem.** Unitree's NVIDIA-backed H2 Plus, the body for NVIDIA's open GR00T reference humanoid running on Jetson Thor, has set off a domestic debate about whether a Chinese chassis carrying American compute and tooling is a win or a dependency. The framing doing the rounds is "the Wintel of robotics", NVIDIA as the standard-setting layer and Unitree as the hardware OEM underneath it. The part nobody in that argument is fighting over is the data the stack runs on. The body and the brain both have loud owners now. The training corpus underneath does not. DigiTimes, who controls body/brain/ecosystem, DigiTimes, the Wintel of robotics

**An Indian deep-tech robotics startup raised a round this week and named the data platform as the thing it is building, not the robot.** Bengaluru's Integra Robotics (Social Hardware International) closed $1.12M pre-Series A led by Finvolve and India Accelerator, and the use of proceeds explicitly includes "its proprietary human-in-the-loop data platform", with data from real-world deployments feeding back to improve the robots. This is the first Indian robotics round in months where the data layer is stated as the asset, not an afterthought bolted onto a hardware pitch. Small cheque, right idea. Indian Startup News, Integra Robotics

**Quiet research day, one consistent thread: the field keeps publishing new ways to pull usable action out of cheap data, because real robot data stays scarce.** LARA jointly trains a latent action model and a VLA so unlabeled human video does more work, and a Blender cloth-manipulation pipeline auto-annotates keypoints because real labeled textile data does not exist. Both are the same admission stated twice. The bottleneck is not models, it is data that survives the conversion into action. LARA arXiv 2606.07100, Cloth keypoints arXiv 2606.06292

days.title: Monday, June 8, 2026
tldr: **Figure ran three humanoids on Helix-02 for over 24 hours of continuous autonomous package sorting, no teleoperation, and crossed 28,000+ packages before stopping.** The test was scoped for 8 hours and kept running. Adcock's line was "over 24 hours of continuous autonomous operation without a failure, this is uncharted territory." The network runs entirely onboard, and the robots self-trigger resets and hand off to each other for maintenance without a human stepping in. Read past the reliability headline. A 24-hour unbroken autonomous run on real packages is a training corpus being written in real time, on Figure's own robots, in Figure's own building. The reliability number is the cover story. The data flywheel is the event. Figure 24h run, Interesting Engineering, Fox News

**NVIDIA dropped GRAIL (arXiv 2606.05160), a pipeline that synthesizes humanoid loco-manipulation data from 3D assets and video foundation-model priors, then verifies it on a real Unitree G1.** It starts from fully specified 3D scenes where geometry, camera, metric scale and a robot-proportioned character are known before any video is generated, builds the interaction in Blender plus Kling AI, reconstructs the 4D human-object trajectory, retargets to the G1 skeleton, and drives a tracking policy in Isaac Lab. There is a public dataset on Hugging Face. Same lesson the spring world-model cluster keeps teaching. The synthetic pipeline only works because it anchors on known 3D structure and real video priors, and it still has to land on a real robot to count. arXiv 2606.05160, NVIDIA GRAIL page

**The American humanoid scale-up is now a manufacturing race, and the bodies are arriving faster than the data to train them.** Figure's BotQ is at one robot per hour, a 24x throughput jump in under 120 days, 350+ Figure 03 units shipped at 80%+ first-pass yield, with output allocated to R&D, data collection and home-task development. 1X's Hayward NEO line is live at 200+ staff, ramping to 10,000 units a year toward 100,000 by end 2027, first-year capacity sold out in five days. The constraint is shifting from can we build the body to can we train it, which is the only part nobody is bidding up. Figure BotQ ramp, 1X NEO Factory

days.title: Friday, June 5, 2026
tldr: **A fresh survey, "From Human Videos to Robot Manipulation" (arXiv 2606.00054), maps the whole field of turning human video into VLA training signal, and it is the cleanest statement yet of why human task video is the substrate everyone is converging on.** It catalogs the four ways the field extracts action information from human video: latent action representations, predictive world models, explicit 2D supervision, and 3D reconstruction. The framing matters for a capture wedge. Robot demonstrations are costly and embodiment-locked, human video is abundant and rich, and the open problem is the transformation step, not the supply. That transformation only works on video captured well enough to recover hands, contacts and 3D structure, which is the validated-against-a-spec point we keep making. arXiv 2606.00054

**CVPR's Embodied AI Workshop ran yesterday in Denver and the real-robot leaderboard is now the field's scorecard, but detailed ManipArena standings have not surfaced publicly yet.** The ARNOLD challenge winners were recognized at the workshop on June 4. The takeaway carries from this week's reporting rather than from fresh standings. The next 12 months of robotics gets argued on real-robot execution across physical tasks, not sim hero numbers. Embodied AI Workshop, ARNOLD Challenge

**Hard news is quiet overnight, which is the honest read for the fourth day running.** The capital sentiment is the loud part. Masayoshi Son told CNBC on June 3 that physical AI and robotics is where he sees the next trillion-dollar company, and CNBC ran a humanoid-as-next-AI-trade segment the same day. The standing tape carries underneath. Unitree's STAR Market clearance, NVIDIA's H2 Plus reference humanoid shipping October, the Surge / Mercor / Scale annotation tape, and the BMW Figure 02 Spartanburg deployment record are all still the background everything reprices against. CNBC on humanoids as the next trade

days.title: Thursday, June 4, 2026
tldr: **CVPR's Embodied AI Workshop runs today and ManipArena is the part that matters, a real-robot manipulation benchmark scoring physical reasoning and generalization across 20 real-world tasks.** It is run with X Square Robot as founding partner and co-initiated by Sun Yat-sen University and MBZUAI in Abu Dhabi, with top teams recognized at the workshop this week. A real-robot leaderboard, not a sim score, is the field saying out loud that the next 12 months of robotics gets decided on physical execution, not benchmark hero numbers. Embodied AI Workshop, X Square Robot launch, PR Newswire

**A fresh TRI-led paper, RoboDream, builds a world model that synthesizes photorealistic robot demonstrations in new scenes and viewpoints and shows it cuts real-world data requirements across manipulation tasks (arXiv 2606.02577, submitted June 1).** Two of its tricks are the tell. "Retrieval and rebirth" reuses old trajectories in entirely new contexts with no new motion data, and "prop-free teleoperation" lets an operator manipulate empty air while the model hallucinates the objects and scene after the fact. This is the synthetic-data line walking straight at captured hours again. arXiv 2606.02577

**Hard news is quiet overnight and the honest read is that most wedges had no fresh move.** The standing tape carries. Unitree's STAR Market clearance and FY2025 numbers, NVIDIA's H2 Plus reference humanoid shipping October, the Surge / Mercor / Scale annotation tape, and the concrete BMW Figure 02 deployment record at Spartanburg are all still the background everything reprices against. Figure at BMW

days.title: Wednesday, June 3, 2026
tldr: **CVPR 2026 opens today in Denver and it is the most embodied conference in the event's history. 4,090 papers accepted out of 16,092 submitted, with physical AI and real-world robotics the dominant theme.** The Embodied AI Workshop runs June 4 and headlines the ManipArena competition, a real-robot manipulation challenge scoring physical reasoning and generalization across 20 real-world tasks. The egocentric world-model cluster is the part to watch this week, papers that turn internet video priors and human ego video into action-conditioned models. For a capture-side wedge this is the week the academic field tells you what a dataset now has to carry. Robotics and Automation News, Embodied AI Workshop

**Unitree's STAR Market clearance is now confirmed, and the full-year 2025 financials in the prospectus reframe the bear case the last two briefs carried.** 2025 revenue ¥1.699B, attributable net profit ¥287.6M, ¥591M after stripping non-recurring share-based-payment expense, and humanoid shipments above 5,500 units for the year, ranked first globally. The Q1 2026 -52% profit line everyone front-paged into the hearing sits against a full year that was clearly profitable. ¥4.2B ($618M) target raise at ~¥42B ($6.2B). Caixin, People's Daily

**The day after GTC Taipei is quiet on hard news, which is the honest read. Most wedges have no fresh move overnight.** The structural tape from the last 72h stands. NVIDIA's H2 Plus reference humanoid (Unitree chassis, Sharpa hands, Jetson Thor, open Isaac GR00T, shipping October to Ai2, ETH, Stanford and UCSD), the US bill to ban federally-funded labs from Unitree robots, and the Surge AI / Mercor / Scale annotation tape are all still the background everything reprices against. NVIDIA newsroom

days.title: Tuesday, June 2, 2026
tldr: **NVIDIA's first open humanoid reference robot is a Chinese chassis. The Isaac GR00T Reference Humanoid Robot announced at GTC Taipei yesterday is Unitree's H2 Plus, and it ships to Stanford, ETH Zurich, UC San Diego and Ai2 in October.** The build is a Unitree H2 (6ft, 150lb, 31 DOF) plus dual Sharpa Wave five-finger tactile hands (22 DOF, 75 DOF total) running Jetson Thor and the open Isaac GR00T stack. Anyone can buy it. This is the embodiment story of the quarter. The reference robot every Western academic VLA paper trains on for the next year is now a single fixed platform, and it is Chinese hardware running US silicon. NVIDIA newsroom, Unitree PR, CNBC

**Unitree cleared its STAR Market listing review on June 1. China now has its first listed pure-play humanoid robotics company.** The Shanghai Stock Exchange listing committee passed the application, ¥4.202B ($619M) target raise at a ~¥42B ($6.2B) valuation, 73 days from formal acceptance on March 20. The clearance and the NVIDIA reference-robot reveal landed the same morning, which is not a coincidence. Every Chinese humanoid valuation in 2026 now reprices against a liquid public benchmark. Seoul Economic Daily, Caixin, Pandaily

**The US-stack-vs-China-stack framing this brief has carried for two weeks just collapsed into one hybrid stack at the research layer.** US lawmakers have a live bill that would ban federally-funded researchers from using Unitree robots over alleged government and military ties, and NVIDIA's answer is to route every software update for the robot's subsystems through its own chip for authenticity checking. So the reference humanoid for Stanford and UCSD is Chinese, the compute is American, and the security model is a US chip policing Chinese firmware. The clean bifurcation story is dead. For HuPa the read sharpens. When the academic embodiment standardizes on one chassis, the contested layer is the data that trains it, and the distribution gap (Indian hands and environments missing from every public corpus) does not care which flag the chassis flies. CNBC, 933 The Drive

days.title: Monday, June 1, 2026
tldr: **The NVIDIA GTC Taipei keynote lands this morning at 11am Taipei (8:30am IST), and the consensus lead is the N1X laptop SoC, not robotics.** This brief is written before Jensen takes the stage, so the outcome is not in yet. What is now firm in the previews: N1X is a 20-core ARM CPU paired with an integrated Blackwell GPU at 6,144 CUDA cores, up to 128GB LPDDR5X, 180-200 AI TOPS, the first chip NVIDIA designed to live inside a laptop instead of a rack. The physical-AI block sits behind it. Jetson Thor deep-dives (2,070 FP4 TFLOPs, 40-130W), GR00T N2, Cosmos Reason 2. Anything Jensen commits to on Jetson Thor + GR00T this morning is what every robotics deck dated after June 2 quotes. ChatForest preview, NVIDIA keynote page, VideoCardz

**Unitree's STAR Market listing-committee hearing is today, the 31st listing-review meeting of 2026, and like the keynote the result is not public at run time.** ¥4.2B ($619M) target raise at ~¥42B ($6.2B) valuation, 73 days from formal acceptance to review. The bear case is on the front page going in. Q1 2026 adjusted net profit -52% YoY to ¥40.3M on revenue +68%. Shoucheng Holdings (697.HK) holds 3.8%, so a clean clearance opens a listed-proxy revaluation window before the IPO itself prices. First listed-pure-play humanoid benchmark in China gets set this morning, same 12-hour window as Jensen. Global Times, Caixin, SCMP

**Quiet overnight on hard news, loud on narrative. Jensen's "$40 trillion TAM for humanoid labor" line is now the dominant institutional framing going into the keynote, with Wall Street rotating into physical-AI names on the back of it.** This is a re-announcement, he has repeated the $40T number for a year, but the buyside positioning around it the last week is the actual event. For HuPa the read is unchanged. When the TAM number gets that big and that loud, the bottleneck conversation moves from "can the robot work" to "what is it trained on", and the data tier is the part of that $40T nobody on the keynote stage is selling. 24/7 Wall St on the $40T framing

days.title: Saturday, May 30, 2026
tldr: **NVIDIA GTC Taipei is in 2 days (Mon June 1, 11am Taipei / 8:30am IST), and the lead expected reveal is now the N1X ARM laptop SoC.** Jensen at the Taipei Music Center the day before Computex floor opens. The N1X is NVIDIA's first chip designed to live inside a laptop instead of a rack, sitting alongside Vera Rubin NVL72 partner news and the physical-AI block (Jetson Thor deep-dives, GR00T / Cosmos Reason 2 rollout). For HuPa the embedded read is unchanged but sharpening: every robotics deck dated after June 2 quotes whatever Jensen lands on Jetson Thor + GR00T, and the same keynote morning is the Unitree listing hearing. Two of the most-watched physical-AI events of Q2 happen inside the same 12 hours. NVIDIA GTC Taipei keynote, VideoCardz, ChatForest preview

**PointWorld lands as the most relevant paper of the weekend, and it is a direct bear signal for raw captured-hours pitches.** A pre-trained 3D world model that represents actions as 3D point flows instead of per-embodiment joint commands, trained on ~2M trajectories / 500 hours across a Franka and a bimanual humanoid, running real-time MPC at 0.1s. The headline claim: a real Franka does rigid-body pushing, deformable and articulated manipulation, and tool use zero-shot from a single in-the-wild RGB-D image, no demos, no post-training. CVPR 2026 highlight. Chris Paxton flagged it 11h ago on the spatial-understanding angle. If single-image-to-action holds at field scale, the value of a captured dataset shifts from hours-collected toward cross-embodiment portability and validated yield, which is exactly the HuPa wedge. arXiv 2601.03782, project page, Chris Paxton on X

**Unitree IPO hearing confirmed for Monday June 1, the 31st STAR Market listing-review meeting of 2026.** ¥4.2B ($619M) target raise at ~¥42B ($6.2B) valuation, 73 days from formal acceptance to review. Q1 2026 adjusted net profit -52% YoY to ¥40.3M on revenue +68%. New wrinkle this week: Shoucheng Holdings (697.HK) holds a 3.8% stake, so a clean clearance opens a public revaluation window for a listed proxy on Unitree before the IPO itself prices. First listed-pure-play humanoid benchmark in China sets Monday morning, same keynote slot as Jensen. Global Times, Caixin, SCMP

days.title: Friday, May 29, 2026
tldr: **Unitree Robotics filed its STAR Market listing-committee hearing for June 1, with Q1 2026 adjusted net profit down 52% on revenue up 68%.** Target raise ¥4.2B ($619M) at a ¥42B ($6.2B) valuation. 73 days from formal application to review. Q1 revenue 422.8M yuan, adjusted net excluding non-recurring 40.3M yuan vs 84.8M a year ago. Q1 humanoid sales 1,200 units. Company attributed the profit pressure to surging R&D plus a sales price war. First-half 2026 guide is +35.6% to +45.4% revenue growth, with profit decline narrowing to between -6.4% and -22%. This is the first listed-pure-play humanoid robotics IPO benchmark, and it lands Monday morning with the bear case already on the front page. Caixin, SCMP, TrendForce, Pandaily

**Robotics Summit Boston wrapped May 28.** State of Humanoids panel at 10am ET landed the framing that humanoid feasibility is no longer the question, standards and deployment architecture are. Confirmed line-up on stage: Alberto Rodriguez (Atlas director of robot behavior, Boston Dynamics), Pras Velagapudi (Agility CTO), Aaron Prather (ASTM), plus Schaeffler and RealSense. The Robot Report's wrap confirms electric Atlas is now "in commercial shipment to Hyundai and Google DeepMind", which is the cleanest public confirmation that the Hyundai 25K commitment has begun. Amazon Vulcan named RBR50 Robot of the Year. Show closed with Noland Arbaugh, the first human Neuralink BCI recipient, moving physical chess pieces by thought on stage to a standing ovation from 5,000+ attendees. Robot Report Day 1 guide, Robot Report Day 2 guide, TechTimes wrap, TechTimes Arbaugh detail

**NVIDIA GTC Taipei keynote is in 3 days, Mon June 1 at 11am Taipei time (8:30am IST).** Jensen Huang at the Taipei Music Center, day before Computex floor opens. Vera CPU + Jetson Thor + GR00T N1.6 / N1.7 + Cosmos Reason 2 rollout numbers are what every series-A robotics deck dated after June 2 will quote. The keynote also lands the same morning as the Unitree STAR Market hearing, which means two of the most-watched physical-AI events in 2026 happen on the same day. NVIDIA GTC Taipei keynote page, NVIDIA blog preview, Computex blog

days.title: Wednesday, May 27, 2026
tldr: **Figure signed a commercial humanoid deal with Catalyst Brands (May 26).** Catalyst operates JCPenney, Aéropostale, Brooks Brothers, Lucky Brand and Nautica across 1,800 stores. Initial deployment is Catalyst's Reno, Nevada distribution centre, working the Joey Pouch sorting system. This is the first western humanoid customer announcement outside automotive / logistics-pure-play / hyperscaler. Department-store apparel supply chains are now a live humanoid SOW. JCPenney Newsroom, Figure announcement, Figure on X

**Hark closed a $700M Series A at $6B post-money (May 21).** Parkway VC led. Cap table: NVIDIA, AMD Ventures, ARK Invest, Brookfield, Greycroft, Intel Capital, Prime Movers Lab, Qualcomm Ventures, Salesforce Ventures, Tamarack. Brett Adcock is running Hark while staying CEO of Figure. NVIDIA + AMD + Intel + Qualcomm on the same cap table is the structural read. Hark is "AI body" Figure's complement on the "AI brain" side. First multimodal models ship this summer, hardware later. TechCrunch, Business Wire, Brett Adcock on X

**Robotics Summit & Expo opens in Boston today at 10am ET.** State of Humanoids panel with Alberto Rodriguez (Boston Dynamics, Atlas director of robot behavior), Pras Velagapudi (Agility CTO), Aaron Prather (ASTM), plus Schaeffler and RealSense. 6,000 attendees on the floor. First public stage where Rodriguez can be asked about the Hyundai 25K commitment, the Korean Metal Workers Union veto, and the missing permanent CEO at the three-month mark, all in one hour. The framing he lands on today sets the August-September buyside pitch deck cycle. Robotics Summit agenda, Robot Report panel preview, Alberto Rodriguez speaker page

days.title: Tuesday, May 26, 2026
tldr: **Robotics Summit & Expo opens in Boston tomorrow at 10am ET.** Alberto Rodriguez (Director of Robot Behavior for Atlas, Boston Dynamics) headlines the "State of Humanoid Robotics" keynote alongside Agility, ASTM, RealSense, Schaeffler. 6,000 attendees on the floor. This is the first public stage where Rodriguez can be asked about Hyundai 25K, the Korean union veto, and the BD interim-CEO situation in the same hour. Buyside Q&A from this session sets the August-September pitch deck cycle. Agenda, Robot Report preview

**Jensen Huang reframed Vera CPU as a $200B physical-AI + agentic-AI TAM on the Q1 earnings call.** $20B in Vera revenue already booked this year. The framing matters because the rest of the humanoid stack (NEURA / Qualcomm Dragonwing, D-Robotics RDK S600, Black Sesame) just got measured against a single SKU NVIDIA is now positioning as the default. Vera + Jetson Thor + Cosmos + GR00T is the consolidating partner stack. Vera + Rubin NVL72 is the data-centre version. Computex / GTC Taipei keynote on June 1 will set the actual rollout numbers. TechCrunch, CNBC analyst reaction, NVIDIA Vera CPU

**Tesla Optimus Gen 3 reveal pushed again to mid-year. Fremont conversion line begins production "late July or August" with output Musk called "literally impossible to predict".** This is the third Gen 3 slip since Q4 2025. Tesla originally promised 1,000 productive units by end-2025, hit zero. The Optimus benchmark every pitch deck has been using ("Tesla scale, Tesla cost, Tesla timeline") is now visibly slipping by a quarter every quarter, and the western humanoid bloc (Figure, Apptronik, NEURA, BD) is filling that vacuum. Electrek, Teslarati

days.title: Monday, May 25, 2026
tldr: **Robotera (China) closed $200M+ on May 8 led by SF Group, HSG and IDG Capital.** The cap table is the real story. Alongside the financial leads, the round pulled in Alibaba, Lenovo, Geely Capital, BAIC, Dongfeng, Haier, Singtel Innov8, China Unicom-affiliated funds and Woori Venture Partners. That is half the Chinese industrial economy on a single humanoid line item, plus a Singapore strategic and a Korean strategic. Robotera is the only Chinese humanoid maker with a PMF claim across 10+ logistics centres (China Post + SF Group), and it began thousand-unit deliveries in Q2. Quietest large round of the quarter, and the most important one for understanding who the Chinese state apparatus has actually picked. PRNewswire, AI Insider

**WIRobotics (Korea) closed a $68M Series B on May 14 and pivoted from wearables to humanoids.** ALLEX is the first humanoid claiming arms-fingers-waist force compliance without force sensors. JB Investment lead, with InterVest, Hana Ventures, Smilegate, SBVA, NH Investment, Company K, GU Investment and FuturePlay in. Revenue jumped from 0.56 → 1.3 → 2.79 billion KRW across 2023-2025 and Q1 2026 alone beat the full 2024 number. They are now joint NVIDIA + AWS partners on the physical-AI side. ALLEX is the Korean alternative to Atlas if Boston Dynamics inventory stays locked to Hyundai for the rest of the decade. PRNewswire, AI Insider

**Pelican-Unified 1.0 paper (arXiv 2605.15153) drops a unified embodied foundation model.** Single VLM serves as the understanding module, reasoning module, and conditioning signal into a Unified Future Generator that jointly denoises future video + future actions. Language, video and action losses all backprop into the shared representation. This is the academic counter-architecture to the modular VLA stack that Pi-0.7, Helix-02, and GR00T-N1.6 are converging on. Worth a careful read before the next investor conversation on model-side competitive dynamics. arXiv

days.title: Sunday, May 24, 2026
tldr: **Hugging Face shipped LeRobot Humanoid on May 21**, a $2,500 open-source bipedal platform with the full stack: 3D-printable parts, BOM, wiring, runtime, identification tools, sim environments. This is the first credible open hardware reference for the humanoid stack. Distinct from HopeJR / Reachy Mini (which are higher and lower price-points, respectively). Every academic group that wanted to publish on a humanoid now has a build target. HF blog, Robots Digest

**The Korean Metal Workers' Union has put a price on the Atlas standoff**. Internal calc pegs each Atlas at roughly ₩200M / ~$145K, framed explicitly as direct labor-cost displacement. The union has nominated the 2026 summer wage talks as the escalation window. First time a humanoid order has a public per-unit price attached by an organized counterparty, and the number every other auto OEM running a humanoid program is now going to be asked about. TechTimes

**Boston Dynamics is still under interim CEO Amanda McMaster** (CFO promoted to interim Feb 2026 after Rob Playter stepped down). She presented the 25K Atlas Hyundai commitment at JPMorgan Boston on May 19. No permanent CEO has been named at the three-month mark, which is the question every Atlas counterparty should be asking right now. Robot Report, The AI Insider

days.title: Saturday, May 23, 2026
tldr: Hyundai disclosed at a JPMorgan investor session on May 19 that it intends to deploy 25,000+ Atlas humanoids across its US Hyundai + Kia plants, absorbing 83% of the 30,000/yr capacity it is targeting by 2028. The Korean Metal Workers' Union then publicly blocked it, "not a single robot can enter the workplace without labor-management agreement." This is the first humanoid order that has triggered an actual labor-relations standoff, and the union answer here will set the template for every western auto OEM running a humanoid program. Korea Herald, Interesting Engineering, TechTimes (union block, May 22)

Figure closed the 24/7 livestream last night at 6pm PT, on plan, at 200 hours. The 9-day uptime number is now in the record, and it is the humanoid datapoint every Q3 deck is going to anchor on. Yesterday Adcock was at 191hr / 238k packages, the final close-out tape is the new benchmark for the rest of 2026. Brett Adcock (close-out call), Figure — Helix Logistics

Brett Adcock's other company, Hark, closed a $700M Series A at a $6B post-money on May 21. Parkway VC lead, with NVIDIA, AMD Ventures, Intel Capital, Qualcomm Ventures and ARK in. AI-native consumer hardware play, models shipping this summer, then devices. He has now stood up two $6B+ vehicles in the same calendar quarter. Bloomberg, TechCrunch, BusinessWire

days.title: Friday, May 22, 2026
tldr: Figure is shutting down the 24/7 livestream tonight. Brett Adcock posted 5h ago: 9 days, 191 consecutive hours, 238,000 packages, zero downtime, he is calling the close at 200 hours at 6pm PT. The fully-autonomous-uptime SLA is now the headline humanoid datapoint of May 2026. @adcock_brett, @Figure_robot

Singapore-based Doozy Robotics broke cover with a seed round, a $200M+ qualified pipeline and a $144M MOU with a major industrial conglomerate, plus a humanoid pilot live at a US pharma customer. SEA now has a second name (after Quikbot) being talked about as a real industrial humanoid play. TNGlobal, Morningstar release

India's Technology Development Board released the first cheques under the ₹1 lakh crore RDI scheme on May 13. Five deeptech firms in cohort 1, including Noccarc Robotics (IIT Kanpur founders) and Dhruva Space (₹105 Cr for a 500kg satellite platform). 3-4% loans, 15yr repayment. The structural unlock for Indian hardware finally has money behind it. YourStory, Inc42 on EndureAir

days.title: Thursday, May 21, 2026
tldr: Singapore stood up its first physical-AI testbed in Punggol and Nvidia announced a Singapore research hub the same day. Grab, DHL, Certis and Quikbot are first pilots. SEA just became a real venue for embodied data deals. CNBC
Figure pushed past 119 consecutive hours and 149,000 packages on the Helix-02 24/7 livestream. The narrative shifted from "cool demo" to "uptime on a real workcell". Brett Adcock, TechRepublic

Scale Labs published its dense-video-captioning playbook (May 19). The big result is that hand-cropped past/present/future collages beat raw video for VLM annotation, and 82% of failures are wrong-object/destination, not wrong-action. Phase 2 calls for bbox overlays. This is the spec conversation we should be having with them. Scale Labs

monthly_reviews.title: May 2026 review
monthly_reviews.summary: May was the month the captured-hours pitch stopped working. NVIDIA's DreamDojo and the PointWorld paper both showed strong manipulation from a few hundred hours plus the right world model, Genesis AI shipped a $200 capture glove, and Scale
